message:
perf: runtime scheduler, reactor, and timer wheel optimizations

Reduce allocations and improve data structure choices across the runtime hot path:

Scheduler:
- IntrusiveStack::steal_batch now takes &mut Vec<TaskId> out-param instead of
  returning a new Vec, eliminating one allocation per steal attempt. Callers
  (including benchmarks) updated accordingly.
- Stealer::steal_batch_locked takes &Arena (shared ref) instead of &mut Arena
  since it only reads records. Stealer::steal now drops the lock before returning
  to reduce contention.
- Default scheduler capacities increased (64→256, scratch 8→32, max 64→256)
  to reduce reallocation frequency in production workloads.

Reactor:
- Replace hashbrown::HashMap with std::collections::HashMap in io_uring, kqueue,
  macos, and windows reactors. The hashbrown dependency was adding complexity
  without measurable benefit in these I/O-bound paths where map sizes are small.
- io_uring: completion collection changed from Vec to SmallVec<64> to avoid
  heap allocation for typical completion batches.
- IoDriver::process_events returns SmallVec<[Waker; 64]> instead of Vec<Waker>
  to keep waker lists on the stack for common batch sizes.

Timer Wheel:
- Replace custom TimerIdHasher + HashMap<u64, u64> with slab::Slab<u64> for
  the active timer map. Slab provides O(1) insert/remove/lookup with better
  cache locality and no hashing overhead. Timer IDs are now slab indices cast
  to u64, eliminating the separate next_id counter.
- Remove TimerIdHasher and its test since it's no longer needed.

Other:
- ObligationTable::remove: replace retain() with position()+swap_remove()
  for O(1) removal instead of O(n) scan-and-shift.
- RuntimeState: same retain→swap_remove optimization for finalizing_regions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
